behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: A real failure is often in pipeline wiring/order (inventory join/filter applied after ranking, stale availability mapping), which a pure unit mock can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation generation against a seeded local catalog DB with mixed stock states and high relevance scores for out-of-stock items; assert zero out-of-stock products appear in returned IDs.

behaviors.description: Blocked categories in user settings are excluded from results.
behaviors.justification: Risk is incorrect composition between user-settings retrieval and catalog filtering, not just local predicate logic.
behaviors.test_description: Seed user profile with blocked categories and a catalog containing those categories; execute full recommendation service path and assert no returned item category is blocked.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Real failures usually come from feature extraction/weight wiring between history modules and ranker, which unit tests of rank function alone can hide.
behaviors.test_description: Seed browsing events and candidate products where only some match recent browsing features; run end-to-end ranking module stack and assert matched products are ordered above neutral-signal products.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page with stable ordering across repeated page-2 requests when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bugs often appear from DB query semantics (missing deterministic tie-break), which mocks frequently mask.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed seeded data in a local DB, request page 2 twice through the service API and assert identical ordered IDs and correct slice boundaries.

behaviors.description: Serendipity requirement: at least one recommendation comes from an unbrowsed category.
behaviors.justification: This is a policy-level ranking constraint across feature computation and post-processing; isolated unit tests can miss interaction effects.
behaviors.test_description: Seed user browsing history concentrated in a subset of categories, run recommendation generation on a diverse catalog, and assert at least one top-N result is from a category absent in user browsing history.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users receive popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback selection using shared popularity data source when history features are missing.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with no events or purchases, seed popularity statistics, run recommender, and assert non-empty results align with top popular products.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, list position, and timestamp for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: Real defects are in request-to-event mapping and persistence schema/serialization, not just local object construction.
behaviors.test_description: Emit click actions via recommendation endpoint/UI handler against local persistence; verify stored event rows contain exact product ID, rank position, and timestamp within expected clock bounds.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Performance depends on real runtime/OS behavior, memory pressure, indexing, and query execution characteristics that mocks and small in-process tests hide.
behaviors.test_description: Run a performance test in a production-like process with a 100k-product dataset and realistic feature joins; measure p95 latency for recommendation calls and assert it is <200ms.

behaviors.description: After purchase, the purchased product is deprioritized in subsequent recommendations.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-request state-update behavior (purchase ingestion + ranking read path); pure unit tests can miss stale or miswired state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Generate baseline recommendations, record purchase of a recommended product, generate recommendations again with unchanged other signals, and assert purchased product rank drops or exits top-N per rule.

behaviors.description: Results are deterministic for identical user state and no intervening activity.
behaviors.justification: Non-determinism commonly comes from unordered data access/tie handling across modules and datastore, which unit tests on isolated functions often miss.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed seeded state and no writes between calls, invoke recommendation endpoint twice and assert exact ordered list equality.
